title: factory_core is the generic, founder-owned software-factory core
content: factory_core is Jeremy McEntire's own proprietary IP (LICENSE = proprietary; PROVENANCE.md asserts separate, independent shippability). It is generic by construction: it imports nothing target-specific. Every per-target input (repo coordinates, working-agreement docs, compliance rules, role bindings, IdP config) is DATA loaded at runtime through the adapter seams in factory_core/adapters.py, never a code dependency. The dependency arrow points consumer -> factory, never the reverse.

title: No target code in factory_core (enforced by check_core_purity)
content: factory_core/ must import only stdlib + the package itself + an allowlisted third-party set (currently jsonschema); it must contain no un-baselined target-specific token in identifiers/strings; and pyproject.toml must list no target pack as a dependency. scripts/check_core_purity.py enforces all three, fail-closed, wired into make ship. Deleting every target pack must leave the core importable, testable, and green (the portability proof in tests/test_portability.py). Trigger: any change under factory_core/. Action: block on any purity finding.

title: The evidence ledger: content-addressed, hash-chained, SoD-enforcing (factory_core/manifest.py)
content: factory_core/manifest.py is a stdlib-only, append-only ledger of lifecycle transitions. Each entry is content-addressed (SHA-256 of its canonical body) and chained to the prior entry's address (prev_hash), so tampering with any entry breaks the chain at the next link. Every append is fail-closed on segregation of duties: implementer, verifier, and approver must be three distinct identities; any two-role overlap is refused (SegregationError). An optional SegregationPolicy adds alias-canonicalization and approver-is-human resolution. Generalized from the origin target's proven single-manifest ledger pattern (a transferability review flagged that file as stdlib-only, domain-agnostic, copy-as-is); this version adds the append-only hash-chain and strips all domain specifics.

title: Phase 0 scope: core skeleton + purity guard, proven against a synthetic empty target
content: Phase 0 delivers the four core modules (manifest ledger, TargetManifest loader, five adapter Protocol seams, role/capability schema), the executable purity guard + baseline, a synthetic empty target fixture, and a full pytest suite including the portability proof. It is a real, tested foundation, NOT a running portal: the orchestration engine, PRD/spec authoring loops, RBAC/SSO, build/demo, and affinity write-back are later phases per the Factory Portal PRD v2. Open item carried forward: manifest signing / out-of-repo key anchoring (OQ17) is wired as a fail-closed seam (verify_signature + require_signature on the loader) but full PKI/rotation/revocation is deferred.
